q: The refractory period after an action potential serves which function?
options: It allows the neuron to generate a stronger signal for the next impulse
It ensures impulses travel in only one direction by preventing restimulation of already-fired membrane segments
It gives synaptic vesicles time to migrate from the cell body to the axon terminal
It allows myelin to reform around the axon before the next signal
explanation: During the refractory period, Na+ channels are inactivated and cannot reopen; this prevents backward propagation and ensures unidirectional travel of the action potential.
